title: Add --volumes-from flag to podman run and create
body:
podman now supports --volumes-from flag, which allows users
to add all the volumes an existing container has to a new one.

Fixes https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/541

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body:
What happens when the original volume was read/only?  Should you have a test to make sure this works?  
If the second container specifies :ro does it end up read/only
If the second container specifies :Z does it relabel?  Is this really valid in Docker?

@rhatdan according to docker's manpage it says you can change the label, but when I tried it I didn't see a change in the label. With podman I can change the label as well as whether it is rw, or ro.
For podman, I am just reading in the volumes bind mounted in the container given to --volumes-from and just bind mounting the same thing into the current container.

@umohnani8 My fear is you could change the volume to something that would not be use able by the original container.  If we only supported :z or even did it automatically that would be ok.
:Z would change the label to something appropriate for the new container, if the old container was not running with the same MCS Label it will now fail to access the content.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 16ea659) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@umohnani8 Needs rebase

@rhatdan I think this is ready now, PTAL.
If the first container has a volume mounted with "Z", then when creating the second container it will throw an error if "z" is not given in the options to change the label from private to shared.
@umohnani8 Does this work with buildin volumes also?
@rhatdan Don't think so... We'd have to look up the image in use and grab them
@rhatdan should it also add the built-in volumes? This just adds the volumes given by the user in the --volume flag to the first container.
@umohnani8 Check what Docker does, I believe it shares builtin volumes as well.
bot, retest this please
@umohnani8 Any progress on this one?
@rhatdan no, got distracted by other things. Will get back to this today.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 19f5a50) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@umohnani8 Needs a rebase.
Handles built-in volumes now. @rhatdan @mheon PTAL
